arts.ttl: Stoke City vs Watford live - Team news from the bet365 Stadium - Stoke-on-Trent Live
arts.sum: I think tackling is a dying art now and people don’t necessarily do that but people can be in good positions to block and go and win things because they’re strong, they’re powerful, they knock people off the ball but they’re also capable tactically to be in the right position at the right time out of possession to be able to do that. What happens next will depend on the team but there must be little tricks to help make sure the atmosphere is positive, like sprinting, more urgent play or a big tackle.

arts.ttl: No regrets about move to Watford despite injury crisis – Feeling player was expendable
arts.sum: Watford signed goalkeeper Egil Selvik from Udinese in the January transfer window. He arrived on a permanent transfer and since joining he has played four times in the Championship and established himself as the Hornets’ first-choice between the sticks. He’s started the last four games for Tom Cleverley’s side. arts.ttl: Watford ‘in discussions’ with agent over buying 24-year-old – Player keen on permanent move to Hornets
arts.sum: Watford have a desire to sign Yasser Larouci on a permanent transfer, according to Africa Foot. Last season, ES Troyes AC let the left-back go out on loan at Sheffield United, who didn’t exercise the purchase option. He returned to England when the Hornets signed him on another loan deal last summer. His performances have The post Watford ‘in discussions’ with agent over buying 24-year-old – Player keen on permanent move to Hornets appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Stoke City fan, 83, fears being kicked out of bet365 Stadium seat
arts.sum: John Hall has a season ticket in the Franklyn Stand, has supported the club for 76 years, and relies on a lift pass which he was granted three years ago to get to his seat. An 83-year-old Stoke City fan fears being kicked out of his bet365 Stadium seat and having to cancel his season ticket - if he is banned from using a lift.

arts.ttl: Stoke City contract state of play as eight players head towards decision time
arts.sum: Three players who have featured prominently so far under new manager Robins - Enda Stevens, Lewis Baker Michael Rose - are in the last year of their current deals, as well as Lynden Gooch, who is recovering from a calf injury; midfielder Jordan Thompson; youth graduate striker Emre Tezgel; and back-up keepers Frank Fielding and Jack Bonham. Left-back Stevens, aged 34, signed a 12-month extension at the end of last term and has been an underrated influence when fit since joining as a free agent from Sheffield United in the summer of 2023.

arts.ttl: Check what's in the water at Stoke City but keep trust in Mark Robins
arts.sum: Behind that, our nearest wide player should be dropping off to invite the ball to be played to the full-back but that wasn’t happening and Lewis Baker and Andrew Moran were nowhere near the right spots to help Lowe. Instead, Lowe kept dropping deeper because the midfield wasn’t picking up and that allowed the two centre-backs to do what they wanted.

arts.ttl: Stoke City's perfect storm as Mark Robins makes plea to home stands
arts.sum: “You’ve got to put the wood in the door at some point and get that turning point and make it that and clearly at this moment in time that’s probably the biggest challenge – to try to get that consistency from having consistent players, to be able to pick a consistent team rather than chopping and changing all the time. Mark Robins, when asked about trying to find the right balance in midfield and consistency in selection, runs through what he calls ‘the perfect storm’ at Stoke City this season – but ends with an emphatic note of being at ‘a brilliant club that will turn’ as long as it can be navigated.

arts.ttl: Watford set to strike permanent deal for 24-year-old loan man
arts.sum: Watford are set to sign loan man Yasser Larouci from ESTAC Troyes on a permanent deal, according to… The post Watford set to strike permanent deal for 24-year-old loan man appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Stoke City monitoring sickness bug ahead of Watford clash
arts.sum: Mark Robins keeps injury updates close to his chest but mentioned illness a few times in a his pre-match press conference on Friday morning and he was still waiting to get a full briefing of who will be available, who will watch from the stands and who might be in their sickbed. Fitness permitting, Ben Pearson, Jordan Thompson and Bosun Lawal are options to be brought into midfield while Eric Bocat could return to defence after making his comeback from a calf injury as a substitute on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Watford host holders Manchester City in the FA Youth Cup semi-finals
arts.sum: The Under-18s, coached by Matt Bevans and Lloyd Doyley, have already claimed the scalps of two Premier League clubs to reach the last four.
